channel.title: CSOH - Cloud Security News
channel.description: Latest cloud security news curated by Cloud Security Office Hours. Covers AWS, Azure, GCP, Kubernetes vulnerabilities, breaches, and more.

item.title: Webinar: Fixing the gaps in network incident response
item.description: IT teams often struggle to quickly coordinate responses across disparate systems during network incidents. This upcoming webinar explores how automation and AI-assisted workflows can reduce response times and help pre...

item.title: Signal adds security warnings for social engineering, phishing attacks
item.description: Signal has introduced new in-app confirmations and warning messages as additional safeguards against phishing and social engineering attempts that could lead to various forms of fraud. [...]

item.title: Microsoft May 2026 Patch Tuesday: Many fixes, but no zero-days
item.description: Microsoft has marked May 2026 Patch Tuesday by releasing fixes for 120+ CVE-numbered vulnerabilities, none of which (for a change) are actively exploited or have been publicly disclosed. Still, some deserve more consi...

item.title: Microsoft releases Windows 10 KB5087544 extended security update
item.description: Microsoft has released the Windows 10 KB5087544 extended security update to fix the May 2026 Patch Tuesday vulnerabilities and resolve an issue with the new Remote Desktop warnings. [...]

item.title: Microsoft May 2026 Patch Tuesday, (Tue, May 12th)
item.description: Today&#;x26;#;39;s Microsoft patch Tuesday fixes 137 different vulnerabilities. In addition, the update addresses 137 Chromium-related issues affecting Microsoft Edge.

item.title: Fortinet warns of critical RCE flaws in FortiSandbox and FortiAuthenticator
item.description: Fortinet has released security patches for two critical vulnerabilities in FortiSandbox and FortiAuthenticator that could enable attackers to run commands or arbitrary code. [...]

item.title: Windows 11 KB5089549 & KB5087420 cumulative updates released
item.description: Microsoft has released Windows 11 KB5089549 and KB5087420 cumulative updates for versions 25H2/24H2 and 23H2 to fix security vulnerabilities, bugs, and add new features. [...]

item.title: Microsoft Patches 137 Vulnerabilities
item.description: Fresh security updates resolve critical flaws in Azure, Windows, Dynamics 365, and the SSO Plugin for Jira & Confluence. The post Microsoft Patches 137 Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Exaforce Raises $125 Million for Agentic SOC Platform
item.description: Exaforce has raised a total of $200 million and plans on using the latest investment for product development and international expansion. The post Exaforce Raises $125 Million for Agentic SOC Platform appeared first o...

item.title: New Exim BDAT Vulnerability Exposes GnuTLS Builds to Potential Code Execution
item.description: Exim has released security updates to address a severe security issue affecting certain configurations that could enable memory corruption and potential code execution. Exim is an open-source Mail Transfer Agent (MTA)...

item.title: Instructure took a risky approach to recover stolen Canvas data
item.description: Instructure, the company behind the online learning platform Canvas, said it reached an agreement with the extortion group ShinyHunters to prevent data stolen in a recent breach from being leaked online. According to...

item.title: New TrickMo Variant Uses TON C2 and SOCKS5 to Create Android Network Pivots
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new version of the TrickMo Android banking trojan that uses The Open Network (TON) for command-and-control (C2). The new variant, observed by ThreatFabric between January and F...

item.title: Mini Shai-Hulud Worm Compromises TanStack, Mistral AI, Guardrails AI & More Packages
item.description: TeamPCP, the threat actor behind the recentsupply chain attack spree, has been linked to the compromise of the npm and PyPI packages from TanStack, UiPath, Mistral AI, OpenSearch, and Guardrails AI as part of a fresh...

item.title: Attackers exploit cPanel CVE-2026-41940 to deploy Filemanager Backdoor
item.description: Attackers are exploiting cPanel flaw CVE-2026-41940 to install the Filemanager backdoor and gain unauthorized admin access. Cybercriminals are actively exploiting the critical cPanel vulnerability CVE-2026-41940 (CVSS...

item.title: OpenAI Launches Daybreak for AI-Powered Vulnerability Detection and Patch Validation
item.description: OpenAI has launched Daybreak, a new cybersecurity initiative that brings together frontier artificial intelligence (AI) model capabilities and Codex Security to help organizations identify and patch vulnerabilities be...

item.title: Škoda warns of customer data breach after online shop hack
item.description: Škoda Auto, a wholly owned subsidiary of the Volkswagen Group, has disclosed a data breach after attackers hacked its online shop and stole the personal information of an undisclosed number of customers. [...]

item.title: Android 17 to expand banking scam call and privacy protections
item.description: Android 17, expected to roll out next month, will introduce several security and privacy features focused on device theft, threat detection, and banking scam calls. [...]

item.title: Adobe Patches 52 Vulnerabilities in 10 Products
item.description: While none of the flaws have been exploited in the wild, many of them could lead to arbitrary code execution. The post Adobe Patches 52 Vulnerabilities in 10 Products appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: SAP unveils Autonomous Enterprise for AI-driven business operations
item.description: SAP introduced the Autonomous Enterprise to help enhance the world’s most critical business workflows, so that humans and AI work together to meet the accelerating demands of global business profitably, strategically...

item.title: AI Agents Security for Developers: Don't Let Your Agents Become a Liability
item.description: Using Cursor, GitHub Copilot, Claude Code, Codex, or another coding agent means giving software access to more than your code. It can also see the credentials available in your workspace, shell, config files, and deve...

item.title: White Circle Raises $11 Million for AI Control Platform
item.description: The startup will invest in accelerating product development, hiring new talent, and expanding its customer base. The post White Circle Raises $11 Million for AI Control Platform appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Enabling AI sovereignty on AWS
item.description: Cloud and AI are transforming industries and societies at unprecedented speed, from accelerating research and enhancing customer experiences to optimizing business processes and enriching public services. At Amazon We...

item.title: Amazon Quick authorization bypass let users reach blocked AI chat agents
item.description: Enterprises running Amazon Quick, the AWS business intelligence and agentic AI service, rely on a feature called custom permissions to restrict who inside an account can use AI chat agents. Fog Security founder Jason...

item.title: Veeam Intelligent ResOps unifies data context and recovery
item.description: Veeam Software announced Veeam Intelligent ResOps, a new solution that unifies data context and recovery operations. As agentic AI accelerates change at machine speed, Intelligent ResOps gives teams the insight they n...

item.title: US bank reports itself after slinging customer data at 'unauthorized AI app'
item.description: Volume and sensitivity of the data cited as chief concerns

item.title: The world’s most “Dangerous” AI, Anthropic’s Mythos, found only one flaw in curl
item.description: Anthropic’s AI found five vulnerabilities in curl, but only one low-severity issue proved to be a real vulnerability. In April, Anthropic made considerable noise announcing Mythos, a new artificial intelligence model...

item.title: RubyGems Suspends New Signups After Hundreds of Malicious Packages Are Uploaded
item.description: RubyGems, the standard package manager for the Ruby programming language, has temporarily paused account sign ups following what has been described as a "major malicious attack." "We're dealing with a major malicious...

item.title: BWH Hotels Says Hackers Had Access to Reservation Data for 6 Months
item.description: Threat actors obtained names and contact information for an unspecified number of BWH Hotels guests. The post BWH Hotels Says Hackers Had Access to Reservation Data for 6 Months appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Hugging Face Packages Weaponized With a Single File Tweak
item.description: A tokenizer library file present in Hugging Face AI models can be manipulated to hijack the model's outputs and exfiltrate data.

item.title: Free OnlyFans Lure Used to Spread Cross-Platform CRPx0 Malware
item.description: CRPx0 is a complex, stealthy malware campaign that targets macOS and Windows systems, and appears to have Linux capabilities in development. The post Free OnlyFans Lure Used to Spread Cross-Platform CRPx0 Malware appe...

item.title: General Motors to pay $12.75 million over driver data sales
item.description: General Motors has agreed to a $12.75 million settlement with California over allegations that it unlawfully sold drivers’ location and behavioral data to brokers, marking the largest penalty in the history of the sta...

item.title: Introducing Wiz Audit History: Track Every Change Across your Environment
item.description: Wiz Audit History is now GA, providing a continuous, cross-cloud timeline of changes to resource configurations and findings to accelerate incident response and simplify compliance.

item.title: How Rapid7 is bringing Cyber GRC closer to security operations
item.description: Sabeen Malik is VP, Global Government Affairs and Public Policy at Rapid7. ⠀ Security teams need a better way to connect what they detect, what they fix, and what they can prove. The pace of modern security operations...

item.title: Download: The IT and security field guide to AI adoption
item.description: Security and IT teams are under pressure to adopt AI, but many are seeing the opposite of what was promised. Tools that demo well don’t hold up in real workflows. Complexity increases. Trust breaks down. And instead o...

item.title: West Pharmaceutical Services Hit by Disruptive Ransomware Attack
item.description: The company took systems offline globally after hackers exfiltrated data and deployed file-encrypting ransomware. The post West Pharmaceutical Services Hit by Disruptive Ransomware Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Apple Patches Dozens of Vulnerabilities in macOS, iOS
item.description: The tech giant has also ported the patch for a recent deleted chats recovery issue to older versions of iOS. The post Apple Patches Dozens of Vulnerabilities in macOS, iOS appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: JetBrains TeamCity vulnerability allows privilege escalation, API exposure (CVE-2026-44413)
item.description: JetBrains has patched a high-severity vulnerability (CVE-2026-44413) in TeamCity, its popular continuous integration and continuous delivery platform, and is urging organizations with on-premises and self-managed depl...

item.title: SAP Patches Critical S/4HANA, Commerce Vulnerabilities
item.description: The flaws could allow attackers to inject malicious code, leading to information disclosure and code execution. The post SAP Patches Critical S/4HANA, Commerce Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: 20 Leaders Who Built the CISO Era: 2 Decades of Change
item.description: As part of Dark Reading's 20th anniversary special coverage, we profile the CISOs, founders, researchers, criminals, and policymakers who rewrote the enterprise risk playbook.

item.title: Attackers Combine ClickFix With PySoxy Proxying to Maintain Persistence
item.description: Exploitation of open-source tools allows attackers to maintain persistent access after initial social engineering, warn ReliaQuest researchers

item.title: Cache-poisoning caper turns TanStack npm packages toxic
item.description: Six-minute supply chain blitz pushed 84 malicious versions with credential theft and disk-wiping code

item.title: Webinar: What the Riskiest SOC Alerts Go Unanswered - and How Radiant Security Can Help
item.description: Why do the Riskiest SOC Alerts Go Unanswered? Security operations teams are drowning in alerts. But the real problem isn't always alert volume; it's the blind spots. The most dangerous alerts are the ones no one is in...

item.title: Citrix moves secure access to a flexible, credit-based consumption model
item.description: Citrix has introduced Citrix Platform Flex, a secure access platform that combines software, management, and infrastructure to deliver managed desktops, enterprise browsing, and zero-trust access in a single offering....

item.title: Claude Mythos Finds Only One Curl Vulnerability; Experts Divided on What It Really Means
item.description: Curl’s lead developer says Mythos claims are marketing, but many in the industry believe the results stem from Curl’s robust security. The post Claude Mythos Finds Only One Curl Vulnerability; Experts Divided on What...

item.title: Copy.Fail Linux Vulnerability
item.description: This is the worst Linux vulnerability in years. TL;DR copy.fail is a Linux kernel local privilege escalation, not a browser or clipboard attack. Disclosed by Theori on 29 April 2026 with a working PoC. It abuses the k...

item.title: SAP fixes critical vulnerabilities in Commerce Cloud and S/4HANA
item.description: SAP has released the May 2026 security updates addressing 15 vulnerabilities across multiple products, including two critical flaws in the Commerce Cloud enterprise-grade e-commerce platform and the S/4HANA ERP suite....

item.title: Is The SOC Obsolete, And We Just Haven’t Admitted It Yet?
item.description: Many AI-first enterprises have already embraced sovereign architectures for general AI initiatives; cybersecurity—and the SOC—should be next. The post Is The SOC Obsolete, And We Just Haven’t Admitted It Yet? appeared...

item.title: Six new dnsmasq vulnerabilities open the door to DNS cache poisoning, local root
item.description: Recent disclosures have revealed that open-source networking tool dnsmasq is grappling with a serious set of vulnerabilities. The problems span memory safety and input validation, with researchers identifying heap buf...

item.title: Why Agentic AI Is Security's Next Blind Spot
item.description: Agentic AI is already running in production environments across many organizations today. It is executing tasks, consuming data, and taking actions — most likely without meaningful involvement from the security team....

item.title: State-sponsored actors, better known as the friends you don’t want
item.description: Responding to a state-sponsored threat is nothing like responding to ransomware, and the differences can make or break the outcome. Learn why your IR plan might need revisiting, and the factors you should consider.

item.title: WannaCry, the ransomware attack that changed the history of cybersecurity
item.description: WannaCry showed how unpatched flaws and leaked cyber tools can cripple global systems, reshaping cybersecurity defenses worldwide. In memory of the day the digital world was shaken, but learned to fight back. The Wann...

item.title: Malicious Hugging Face Repository Typosquats OpenAI
item.description: HiddenLayer reveals infostealer malware in a Hugging Face repository

item.title: South Staffordshire Water Fined £1m After Data Breach
item.description: The ICO has fined South Staffordshire Water nearly £1m for a series of data protection failings

item.title: Japan’s PM orders cybersecurity review to stop Mythos going full CyberZilla
item.description: Fears exponential increase in attack scale and speed

item.title: HEIDI: Free IDE security plugin for open-source vulnerability checks
item.description: Open-source dependencies make up a large percentage of the code in production applications, and most vulnerability checks still run late in the pipeline, inside CI/CD systems or after a release ships. Meterian is movi...

item.title: The hidden smart fridge risks that emerge years after purchase
item.description: Household refrigerators are built to last more than a decade. The software, cloud services, and mobile apps that control them are not. A new analysis from Erik Buchmann at Leipzig University maps what happens when tho...

item.title: ISC Stormcast For Tuesday, May 12th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9928, (Tue, May 12th)
Elastic Security MCP App: Interactive security operations inside your AI Tools
item.description: Elastic Security is the first security vendor to ship an interactive UI in AI tools. Triage alerts, hunt threats, correlate attack chains, and open cases, all from inside your AI conversation.

item.title: Introducing Prempti: Runtime security for AI coding agents, powered by Falco
item.description: AI coding agents like Claude Code and Codex have become a natural part of the developer workflow. You give them a task and they read your files, run commands, make network requests, and write code, all on your behalf...

item.title: CVE-2026-44338: PraisonAI authentication bypass in under 4 hours and the growing trend of rapid exploitation
Inside AD CS Escalation: Unpacking Advanced Misuse Techniques and Tools
item.description: Unit 42 analyzes AD CS exploitation through template misconfigurations and shadow credential misuse while offering behavioral detection for defenders. The post Inside AD CS Escalation: Unpacking Advanced Misuse Techni...

item.title: TeamPCP Compromises Checkmarx Jenkins AST Plugin Weeks After KICS Supply Chain Attack
item.description: Checkmarx has confirmed that a modified version of the Jenkins AST plugin was published to the Jenkins Marketplace. "If you are using Checkmarx Jenkins AST plugin, you need to ensure that you are using the version 2.0...

item.title: Hackers Used AI to Develop First Known Zero-Day 2FA Bypass for Mass Exploitation
item.description: Google on Monday disclosed that it identified an unknown threat actor using a zero-day exploit that it said was likely developed with an artificial intelligence (AI) system, marking the first time the technology has b...

item.title: Identity security firm SailPoint discloses GitHub repository breach
item.description: SailPoint disclosed a GitHub repository breach on April 20. The company contained the incident and said no customer data was affected. SailPoint is a cybersecurity company that provides identity security and identity...

item.title: 'Dirty Frag' Exploit Poised to Blow Up on Enterprise Linux Distros
item.description: The privilege escalation vulnerability, which is similar to other Linux flaws like Copy Fail and Dirty Pipe, may already be under limited exploitation.

item.title: Double Canvas breach acknowledged as ShinyHunters sets new pay-or-leak deadline
item.description: UPDATED: Sorry, kids, everything's back up so get to work on your new assignment - An essay on the ethics of paying ransoms, because it looks like that's what happened here

item.title: Apple Patches Everything, (Mon, May 11th)
item.description: Apple today released its typical feature update across it&#;x26;#;39;s operating systems (iOS, iPadOS, macOS, tvOS, watchOS, vision OS). With this update, Apple patched 84 different vulnerabilities. Updates are availa...

item.title: iOS 26.5 is out, bringing encrypted RCS messaging to iPhone and Android users
item.description: Apple is bringing long-awaited end-to-end encryption to Rich Communication Services (RCS) messaging between iPhone and Android users in iOS 26.5. The feature is launching in beta for iPhone users running iOS 26.5 on s...

item.title: Resurrected ‘Crimenetwork’ Marketplace Taken Down, Administrator Arrested
item.description: The second iteration of the German-speaking online crime marketplace had over 22,000 users and more than 100 sellers. The post Resurrected ‘Crimenetwork’ Marketplace Taken Down, Administrator Arrested appeared first o...

item.title: New GhostLock tool abuses Windows API to block file access
item.description: A security researcher has released a proof-of-concept tool named GhostLock that demonstrates how a legitimate Windows file API can be abused in attacks to block access to files stored locally or on SMB network shares....

item.title: Tech Can't Stop These Threats — Your People Can
item.description: Security controls can do only so much. Here are four attacks where your employees are usually your first, and only, line of cyber defense.

item.title: Complimentary virtual training: Get hands-on with AWS Security Services
item.description: If you’re looking to strengthen your organization’s security posture on Amazon Web Services (AWS) but aren’t sure where to start, then we’re here to help. Security Activation Days are complimentary, virtual, hands-on...

item.title: Vulnerability Summary for the Week of May 4, 2026
item.description: High Vulnerabilities Primary Vendor -- Product Description Published CVSS Score Source Info Patch Info gotenberg--gotenberg Gotenberg is a Docker-powered stateless API for PDF files. In versions 8.30.1 and earlier, th...

item.title: Frame Security Emerges From Stealth With $50M for Awareness and Training Platform
item.description: Team8, Index Ventures, Picture Capital, Elad Gil, Cerca Partners, and Tesonet invested in Frame Security. The post Frame Security Emerges From Stealth With $50M for Awareness and Training Platform appeared first on Se...

item.title: GitGuardian Now Flags Admin and Overprivileged Identities Across AWS, Entra, and Okta
item.description: GitGuardian's NHI Governance now adds privilege context to leaked secrets, auto-escalating admin-level risks for smarter prioritization across AWS, Entra, and Okta. Discover how admin badges and overprivilege detectio...

item.title: Red Hat extends open source technology into space
item.description: Red Hat and Voyager Technologies announced the successful deployment of Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.1 and Red Hat Universal Base Image (UBI) to Voyager’s LEOcloud Space Edge IaaS Micro Datacenter aboard the Internatio...

item.title: Instructure confirms hackers used Canvas flaw to deface portals
item.description: Education technology giant Instructure has confirmed that a security vulnerability allowed hackers to modify Canvas login portals and leave an extortion message. [...]

item.title: Rushed Patches Follow Broken Embargo on New Linux Kernel Vulnerabilities
item.description: Two new high-severity vulnerabilities, dubbed ’Dirty Frag’ when chained, have been found in the Linux kernel, affecting most Linux distributions

item.title: Why we use CAPTCHAs, (Mon, May 11th)
item.description: A few months ago, I implemented Cloudflare&#;x26;#;39;s Turnstile CAPTCHA on some pages. The reason for implementing these CAPTCHAs is obvious: Bots make up a large percentage of traffic and affect site performance.

item.title: GTIG AI Threat Tracker: Adversaries Leverage AI for Vulnerability Exploitation, Augmented Operations, and Initial Access
item.description: Executive Summary Since our February 2026 report on AI-related threat activity, Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG) has continued to track a maturing transition from nascent AI-enabled operations to the industrial...

item.title: Why Changing Passwords Doesn’t End an Active Directory Breach
item.description: Resetting a password doesn't always remove attackers from Active Directory. Specops Software explains how cached credentials and Kerberos tickets can keep attackers authenticated after a reset. [...]

item.title: Hackers Use AI for Exploit Development, Attack Automation
item.description: Cyber adversaries have long used AI, but now attackers are using large language models to develop exploits and orchestrate complex attacks.

item.title: Wiz at Wiz: Reducing Risk through Service Ownership
item.description: How Wiz security uses Service Catalog to turn cloud risk into service ownership

item.title: Alation AI Governance creates a system of record for AI oversight
item.description: Alation has introduced Alation AI Governance, a new offering that gives enterprises the system of record they are missing for AI compliance. Enterprises are deploying AI models, agents, and tools faster than they can...

item.title: Linux developers weigh emergency “killswitch” for vulnerable kernel functions
item.description: Linux kernel developers are reviewing a proposal for an emergency risk mitigation mechanism (“Killswitch”) that would allow administrators to disable vulnerable kernel functions at runtime. The proposal, submitted by...

item.title: Final Countdown: Last Chance to Join the Rapid7 Global Cybersecurity Summit
item.description: The Rapid7 2026 Global Cybersecurity Summit is just around the corner, and with it, a final opportunity to join the conversations shaping how security teams are adapting to a rapidly changing landscape. Over the past...

item.title: SailPoint Agentic Fabric expands identity governance to autonomous AI agents
item.description: SailPoint has introduced SailPoint Agentic Fabric, a new platform designed to help enterprises secure AI agents and other non-human identities at scale. As organizations deploy autonomous AI agents across cloud enviro...

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Linux Rootkit, macOS Crypto Stealer, WebSocket Skimmers and More
item.description: Rough Monday. Somebody poisoned a trusted download again, somebody else turned cloud servers into public housing, and a few crews are still getting into boxes with bugs that should’ve died years ago — the same old hol...

item.title: Webinar this week: Prevention alone is not enough against modern attacks
item.description: This upcoming webinar explores how organizations need to combine security, backups, and recovery planning to reduce the impact of modern cyberattacks. [...]

item.title: Welcome to the Google Summer of Code 2026!
item.description: On behalf of the entire OWASP Foundation, it is our absolute pleasure to welcome 26 new contributors to Google Summer of Code 2026. This summer, you’re not just writing code, you’re helping build a more secure world,...

item.title: Inside CrowdStrike Automated Leads: A Transformative Approach to Threat Detections
Your Purple Team Isn't Purple — It's Just Red and Blue in the Same Room
item.description: Defending a network at 2 am looks a lot like this: an analyst copy-pasting a hash from a PDF into a SIEM query. A red team script is being rewritten by hand so the blue team can use it. A patch waiting on a change-app...

item.title: Cloudflare Lays Off 1,100 Employees in AI-Driven Restructuring
item.description: The company topped revenue and earnings forecasts for the first quarter of 2026, but its shares plunged more than 20%. The post Cloudflare Lays Off 1,100 Employees in AI-Driven Restructuring appeared first on Security...

item.title: US: FCC Relaxes Foreign-Made Router Ban to Allow for Security Updates
item.description: The same extension applies to security updates shipped to US-based users of foreign-made drones

item.title: The questionnaire-based TPRM model is broken, and TrustCloud has a fix
item.description: TrustCloud announced a new version of TrustLens, its third party risk management (TPRM) solution. The new TrustLens agentic AI capabilities focus on delivering four requirements every CISO wants in their TPRM program:...

item.title: ShinyHunters Escalates Canvas Extortion with School by School Ransom Campaign
item.description: ShinyHunters has escalated its Canvas extortion campaign, defacing hundreds of school login pages and threatening to leak stolen data unless institutions negotiate

item.title: The scam economy has found its AI upgrade
item.description: Scam attempts continue to reach consumers via email, text messages, social media, online advertising, and phone calls. The volume of exposure has remained stable over the past year, with more than half of consumers en...

item.title: U.S. CISA adds a flaw in BerriAI LiteLLM to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) adds a flaw in BerriAI LiteLLM to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) added a...

item.title: TrickMo Android banker adopts TON blockchain for covert comms
item.description: A new variant of the TrickMo Android banking malware, delivered in campaigns targeting users across Europe, introduces new commands and uses The Open Network (TON) for stealthy command-and-control communications. [...]

item.title: Zara Data Breach Impacts Nearly 200,000 Customers
item.description: ShinyHunters gets away with emails and other data on 200,000 Zara customers

item.title: Rustinel: Open-source endpoint detection for Windows and Linux
item.description: Open-source endpoint detection has long been split between Windows-focused tools built around Sysmon and Linux tools built around eBPF or auditd. Defenders running mixed environments have had to stitch together separa...

item.title: Review: Foundations of Cybersecurity, 2nd edition
item.description: Jason Andress has refreshed his introductory security text for No Starch Press. He writes in the introduction that the term security now extends past data center servers to cloud resources, mobile devices, the Interne...

item.title: Security teams are turning to AI to survive alert overload
item.description: The World Economic Forum white paper “Empowering Defenders: AI for Cybersecurity” identified AI as the biggest driver of change in cybersecurity for 94% of survey respondents. The paper found that 77% of organizations...

item.title: Over 500 Organizations Hit in Years-Long Phishing Campaign
item.description: Victims span across the aviation, critical infrastructure, energy, logistics, public administration, and technology sectors. The post Over 500 Organizations Hit in Years-Long Phishing Campaign appeared first on Securi...

item.title: Chainguard brings first-party RHEL 9 and RHEL 10 RPM support to Chainguard OS, joins FINOS
item.description: Chainguard adds first-party RHEL 9/10 RPM compatibility and joins FINOS, helping financial institutions modernize securely for the AI-driven threat era.

item.title: SECURITY AFFAIRS MALWARE NEWSLETTER ROUND 96
item.description: Security Affairs Malware newsletter includes a collection of the best articles and research on malware in the international landscape Malware Newsletter CloudZ RAT potentially steals OTP messages using Pheno plugin Ba...

item.title: Hackers abuse Google ads, Claude.ai chats to push Mac malware
item.description: Attackers are abusing Google Ads and legitimate Claude.ai shared chats in an active malvertising campaign. Users searching for "Claude mac download" may come across sponsored search results that list claude.ai as the...

item.title: Official JDownloader site served malware to Windows and Linux users between May 6 and May 7
item.description: JDownloader website was hacked to distribute malicious Windows and Linux installers carrying a Python RAT between May 6–7, 2026. JDownloader official website was compromised in a supply chain attack that replaced legi...

item.title: Week in review: cPanel vulnerability actively exploited, DigiCert breach, LinkedIn job scams
item.description: Here’s an overview of some of last week’s most interesting news, articles, interviews and videos: Your work apps are quietly handing 19 data points to someone Office work in 2026 relies on mobile apps used alongside p...

item.title: cPanel, WHM Release Fixes for Three New Vulnerabilities — Patch Now
item.description: cPanel has released updates to address three vulnerabilities in cPanel and Web Host Manager (WHM) that could be exploited to achieve privilege escalation, code execution, and denial-of-service. The list of vulnerabili...

item.title: Quasar Linux RAT (QLNX): A Fileless Linux Implant Built for Stealth and Persistence
item.description: Researchers uncovered QLNX, a Linux RAT targeting developers to steal credentials, log keystrokes, monitor systems, and enable remote access. Security researchers discovered a previously undocumented Linux malware cal...

item.title: Critical Apache HTTP Server HTTP/2 Vulnerability Could Enable Remote Code Execution
item.description: A high-severity vulnerability (CVE-2026-23918, CVSS 8.8) was disclosed affecting Apache HTTP Server, allowing attackers to potentially achieve remote code execution via specially crafted HTTP/2 requests. Due to the po...

item.title: Australian Cyber Security Centre Issues Alert Over ClickFix Attacks
item.description: ACSC warns over a campaign targeting organizations which uses ClickFix to deliver Vidar infostealer malware

item.title: Hackers ate my homework: Educational SaaS Canvas down after cyberattack
item.description: ShinyHunters takes the credit and gives developer an F for security

item.title: One Missed Threat Per Week: What 25M Alerts Reveal About Low-Severity Risk
item.description: The dark secret of enterprise security operations is that defenders have quietly institutionalized the practice of not looking. This is not just anecdotal, but rather backed by a recent report investigating more than...

item.title: New Linux PamDOORa Backdoor Uses PAM Modules to Steal SSH Credentials
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new Linux backdoor named PamDOORa that's being advertised on the Rehub Russian cybercrime forum for $1,600 by a threat actor called "darkworm." The backdoor is des...

item.title: One Click, Total Shutdown: The "Patient Zero" Webinar on Killing Stealth Breaches
item.description: The hardest part of cybersecurity isn't the technology, it’s the people. Every major breach you’ve read about lately usually starts the same way: one employee, one clever email, and one "Patient Zero" infection. In 20...

item.title: RansomHouse says it breached Trellix and exposes internal systems
item.description: RansomHouse claimed responsibility for the Trellix breach, adding the security firm to its Tor data leak site and sharing screenshots of internal systems. The RansomHouse ransomware group has claimed responsibility fo...

item.title: Ivanti EPMM vulnerability exploited in zero-day attacks (CVE-2026-6973)
item.description: Ivanti has released fixes for 5 high-severity vulnerabilities in its Endpoint Manager Mobile (EPMM) solution, one of which (CVE-2026-6973) has being exploited as a zero-day by attackers. “We are aware of a very limite...

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Giant Squid Live in the Waters of Western Australia
item.description: Evidence of them has been found by analyzing DNA in the seawater. As usual, you can also use this squid post to talk about the security stories in the news that I haven’t covered. Blog moderation policy.
